dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4729 IS: Biosecurity Smuggling Deterrence Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To impose mandatory minimum sentences for conspiracy to smuggle biological agents into the United States and for making false statements to Federal agents in connection with such smuggling, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Biosecurity Smuggling Deterrence Act of 2026.

section.header: Mandatory minimum sentences for certain offenses involving biological agents

subsection.text: Section 371 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the first undesignated paragraph, by striking If two and inserting the following:

subsection.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, if two

paragraph.text: in the second undesignated paragraph, by striking If, however, and inserting the following:
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Mandatory minimum for biological agent smuggling
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section or any other law, if the offense, the commission of which is the object of the conspiracy, involves the smuggling into the United States, or the attempted smuggling into the United States, of a biological agent or toxin (as defined in section 178), the persons convicted shall be sentenced to a term of imprisonment of not less than 20 years.

subsection.header: False statements in connection with biological agent smuggling
subsection.text: Section 1001 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Mandatory minimum for statements involving biological agents
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, if the matter within the jurisdiction of the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the Government of the United States to which the false statement, concealment, or false writing or document relates involves the smuggling, importation, declaration, permit application, or any other Federal authorization regarding a biological agent or toxin (as defined in section 178), the person convicted shall be sentenced to a term of imprisonment of not less than 5 years.
Section 545 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the first undesignated paragraph, by inserting (a) In general.— before Whoever knowingly and willfully;

in the third undesignated paragraph, by striking the period at the end of the sentence and inserting , unless the offense involves the smuggling or attempted smuggling of biological agents under subsection (e).;

subparagraph.text: by inserting (b) Evidence sufficient for conviction.— before Proof of defendant's possession; and
by striking the first or second paragraph of this section and inserting subsection (a);

paragraph.text: in the fifth undesignated paragraph, by inserting (c) Forfeiture.— before Merchandise introduced;
in the sixth undesignated paragraph, by inserting (d) Definition of United States.— before The term United States; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Mandatory minimum for biological agent smuggling
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section or any other law, if the offense involves the smuggling into the United States, or the attempted smuggling into the United States, of a biological agent or toxin (as defined in section 178), the person convicted shall be sentenced to a term of imprisonment of not less than 20 years.

section.text: The amendments made by this Act shall apply to any offense committed on or after the date of enactment of this Act.
